title: Jest for React Native Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of unit testing, RNTL queries, hook testing, and mocking in React Native.

questions.question: Which RNTL query is preferred over getByTestId and why?

options.text: getByRole — testID attributes are not stable across refactors; role-based and text-based queries describe what the user actually sees and interacts with
getByClassName — it is more stable than text-based queries
getById — native IDs are always stable and don't change
getAllByIndex — accessing components by position is the most reliable approach

questions.question: What does `jest.useFakeTimers()` allow you to do in a test?

options.text: It makes tests run faster by skipping real I/O operations
It replaces real timers with synchronous mock timers, allowing you to control setTimeout/setInterval behavior without waiting real time
It prevents Jest from timing out when a test runs longer than 5 seconds
It mocks the device's system clock to a fixed timestamp

questions.question: In the AAA testing pattern, what does each letter stand for?

options.text: Async, Await, Assert
Arrange, Act, Assert — set up the test environment, perform the action, verify the result
API, Application, Assertion
Authenticate, Authorize, Audit

questions.question: Why should external dependencies like fetch API calls always be mocked in unit tests?

options.text: Real API calls will cause deprecation warnings in Jest
Tests with real network calls are non-deterministic — they can fail due to network issues, server downtime, or rate limits, making the test suite unreliable
Jest does not have access to the network on any platform
Mocking is mandatory because React Native overrides the global fetch function

questions.question: What is the primary difference between `renderHook` and `render` in React Native Testing Library?

options.text: renderHook can only be used for hooks that manage state; render is for hooks with side effects
renderHook is used to test custom hooks in isolation without rendering a full component; render is for testing full component trees
renderHook renders components without the React Native bridge; render uses the full bridge
They are identical; renderHook is just an alias for render that filters out JSX output
